summary: 1980 horror film directed by Stanley Kubrick, adapted from Stephen King's novel. Jack Torrance (Jack Nicholson) serves as winter caretaker of the isolated Overlook Hotel in Colorado, where he goes mad and pursues his family — wife Wendy (Shelley Duvall) and son Danny — with an axe. Famous for 'Here's Johnny!', the tricycle, and the twins scene. Score assembled from Penderecki, Wendy Carlos/Rachel Elkind, Bartok, and Ligeti.

works.name: Score and Music
works.description: The Shining's score is a collage of pre-existing classical works. The main theme was created by Wendy Carlos and Rachel Elkind, adapting the 'Dream of a Witches' Sabbath' from Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique. Penderecki's tone cluster-heavy pieces are used extensively: Utrejna, Polymorphia, The Awakening of Jacob, and two pieces titled De Natura Sonoris. Bartok's 'night music' excerpts play during a crane shot. Ligeti's Lontano was used in the film. A Penderecki piece plays when Danny turns the corner on his tricycle and sees the identical twins.

clues.clue: Main theme by Wendy Carlos and Rachel Elkind adapts the 'Dream of a Witches' Sabbath' from Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique
examples: This film's theme, which adapts the 'Dream of a Witches' Sabbath' from Symphonie Fantastique, was made by Rachel Elkind and Wendy Carlos.

clues.clue: Penderecki's Utrejna, Polymorphia, and The Awakening of Jacob are used; two pieces titled De Natura Sonoris also appear
examples: This film uses two pieces titled De Natura Sonoris.
Though it does not star Linda Blair, parts of the tone cluster-heavy pieces Utrejna, Polymorphia, and The Awakening of Jacob were used in this film.

clues.clue: A Penderecki piece plays when the bigwheel rider (Danny) turns the corner to see the identical twins; a Bartok 'night music' excerpt plays during a crane shot transitioning between a model and what it represents
examples: A piece by Penderecki plays in this film when a bigwheel rider turns the corner to see identical twins.
A crane shot transitions between a model and the object it represents in a part of this film that uses an excerpt of 'night music' by Bela Bartok.

clues.clue: Ligeti's Lontano appears on the soundtrack
examples: A Gyorgy Ligeti piece of this name appears on the soundtracks of Shutter Island and The Shining.

works.name: Iconic Scenes and Imagery
works.description: Key scenes: the tricycle sequence (floor-level tracking shots follow Danny on a blue-seated tricycle through the hotel; he sees a torrent of blood from an elevator and the identical twins — inspired by a Diane Arbus photograph). The Gold Room scene ('Midnight, the Stars and You' plays as Jack anachronistically appears in a 1921 party photo). The bathroom scene (red walls, white ceiling/floor; a man wipes Jack's jacket and mentions 'correcting' his daughters). The axe scene (Jack breaks through a door; 'Here's Johnny!'). The axe scene was influenced by Victor Sjostrom's Swedish silent film The Phantom Carriage (1921).

clues.clue: Danny rides a blue-seated tricycle; floor-level tracking shots follow him through the hotel; he sees a torrent of blood gushing from an elevator
examples: This film repeatedly uses floor-level tracking shots to follow a boy on a blue-seated tricycle through a building in which he has a vision of a torrent of blood gushing from an elevator.

clues.clue: The twin girls scene was inspired by a Diane Arbus photograph of identical twins in matching outfits
examples: A photographer from this city, Diane Arbus, inspired the twin girls scene in The Shining
Two of these people in matching outfits captured in a Diane Arbus photo inspired a similar set of twins in The Shining.

examples: In this movie, 'Midnight, the Stars and You' plays as a slow zoom into the Gold Room shows the main character anachronistically appear in a photo of a 1921 party.

works.name: Production / Novel / Legacy
works.description: Stanley Kubrick film (1980), adapted from Stephen King's novel. The Stanley Hotel in Colorado inspired the novel. In the novel, Dick Halloran gets a distress call and rushes to Colorado but is attacked by topiaries (in the film he is killed by Jack). Shelley Duvall played Wendy; she denied being professionally abused by Kubrick. The film is set at the Overlook Hotel. Kubrick is credited with having influenced subsequent horror film music through this film. The film is analyzed in Mark Fisher's book The Weird and the Eerie.

clues.clue: Directed by Stanley Kubrick; adapted from Stephen King's novel; set at the Overlook Hotel in Colorado; the Stanley Hotel in Colorado inspired the novel
examples: name this Stanley Kubrick film set in a hotel in Colorado, which influenced the music of subsequent horror films.
the Stanley Hotel in this state inspired Stephen King's novel The Shining

clues.clue: Shelley Duvall played Wendy; she denied being professionally abused by Kubrick during filming

examples: In one of this author's novels, the chef Dick Halloran gets a distress call from another character, causing him to rush to Colorado, where he is attacked by topiaries.

comprehensive_summary:
The Shining (1980) is a horror film directed by Stanley Kubrick, adapted from Stephen King's novel (which was inspired by the Stanley Hotel in Colorado). Jack Torrance (Jack Nicholson) becomes the winter caretaker of the Overlook Hotel, where he goes mad. His wife Wendy (Shelley Duvall) and son Danny (who rides a blue-seated tricycle through the hotel in floor-level tracking shots) face his increasing violence. Danny sees a torrent of blood gushing from an elevator and the famous identical ghost twins — the twins scene was inspired by a Diane Arbus photograph of two girls in matching outfits. Jack pursues his family with an axe, breaking through a door with 'Here's Johnny!'; he ends up frozen to death in a hedge maze. The axe scene drew on Victor Sjostrom's 1921 Swedish silent film The Phantom Carriage. The Gold Room scene features 'Midnight, the Stars and You' playing as the camera slowly zooms to reveal Jack anachronistically in a 1921 party photograph. A bathroom scene features red walls with white ceiling and floor, where a man wipes Jack's jacket while mentioning how he 'corrected' his daughters. In the novel, chef Dick Halloran receives a distress call and rushes to Colorado where he is attacked by topiaries (in the film he is simply killed). Shelley Duvall denied being professionally abused by Kubrick during filming.

The film's score is a collage of classical works. Wendy Carlos and Rachel Elkind created the main theme adapting the 'Dream of a Witches' Sabbath' from Berlioz's Symphonie Fantastique. Penderecki's tone cluster-heavy works are used extensively: Utrejna, Polymorphia, The Awakening of Jacob, and two pieces titled De Natura Sonoris. A Penderecki piece plays when Danny turns the corner on his tricycle to see the twins. Bartok's 'night music' plays during a crane shot transitioning between a model and what it represents. Ligeti's Lontano also appears. Wendy Carlos also scored A Clockwork Orange for Kubrick.
recursive_suggestions: Stanley Kubrick — director; many films with own answerline clues (Fine Arts)
Wendy Carlos — electronic music composer; scored both The Shining and A Clockwork Orange for Kubrick (Fine Arts)
Stephen King — author of the source novel (Literature)
